title: Computational and Experimental Development of a Novel Multi-Epitope Vaccine Candidate Against Bovine Leukaemia Virus.
snippet: Bovine leukaemia virus (BLV) is the causative agent of enzootic bovine leukosis, a chronic infectious disease that causes significant economic losses in the dairy and beef industries worldwide. Despite extensive research, there is no licensed vaccine available for effective prevention and control of BLV infection. This study aimed to design and evaluate a n...

title: Reducing Unmet Needs in Hidradenitis Suppurativa by Including the Hair Follicle Among an Arsenal of Targets.
snippet: Hidradenitis suppurativa (HS) is a prevalent, debilitating disease affecting ~1% of the population, with limited therapeutic options and poor long-term remission rates. HS arises from inflammation and destruction of apocrine-bearing hair follicles, suggesting a multifactorial pathology involving immune dysregulation and follicular dysfunction. Emerging trea...

title: Multi-omics analysis identifies altered EOMES-associated differentiation programs of precursor effector-memory CD8⁺ T cells in transplant-associated cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma.
snippet: Transplant-associated cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (TSCC) is one of the most common malignancies in solid organ transplant recipients and is strongly associated with long-term immunosuppression. However, the transcriptional programs and molecular mechanisms through which chronic immunosuppression reshapes intratumoral CD8⁺ T-cell differentiation...

title: MET-amplified gastric cancers exhibit co-amplifications of BRAF, CDK6, and EGFR.
publicationName: The journal of pathology. Clinical research
journalName: The journal of pathology. Clinical research
snippet: MET-positive gastric cancer (GC) has a poor prognosis. In addition, targeting MET with monoclonal antibodies or tyrosine kinase inhibitors had limited success. We searched for additional genetic alterations that might explain the poor outcome of MET-positive GC and lack of response to MET-targeted therapies.
